name: Ubral
description:
Ubral

Key Demographics: Humans Capital City: Dùn nan Clach

*{Entanglements

description[leftmargin=*, style=nextline] [To the West (via Viterra):] Vhasia -- The mountain passes funnel exiles and mercenaries; the Sun Court's Viterran entanglements offers opportunities for the saavy laird. [To the South:] Viterra -- The lowland tax collectors and the Queen's justiciars claim suzerainty; the clans call it "ink on wet stone." The cairns vote against the crown every season. [To the North:] Aeler -- The exiled dwarves of Khaz-Vurim maintain the toll-roads; the mountain's breath is counted in iron. The under-passages are a source of wealth and danger. [To the East:] The Dolmis Sea -- Fishing villages and hidden coves trade in salt and secrets; the sea is a horizon, not a border. The cairns do not face the water; the dead do not swim. description

*{Quick Reference Tagline: The lowlands write their laws in ink. We carve ours in stone. The ink fades. The stone remembers.

quote Lady of Tor (Elite): "The stone here remembers every oath sworn upon it and every blood spilled in its shadow. To lead is to understand that the land itself is the true sovereign, and we are merely its stewards. The lowland kings write their claims on parchment. We write ours in cairns. And when the ink fades, as it always does, the cairns will still be here---waiting." quote

quote Hearth-Aunt (Commoner): "Hospitality's a sacred thing in these hills---break bread at my table and you're family till the sun sets twice. But cross that trust and the very stones will remember your name. The Viterran tax men learned that. Their bones are under the sheepwalk now. We don't forget. We don't forgive. We weigh every slight in cattle and count every insult in winters." quote

Genre / Mood: Highland clan honor, misty glens, wergild and blood feuds, exiled dwarves, geometry of grudges, Scottish folk horror.

Starting Location: A Warden's Cairn at the fog line. Spears planted like grave markers, their shafts wrapped in faded clan ribbons. Hoofbeats echo in the valley below. The Hearth-Aunt offers a cup of small beer and a piece of oatcake. "The Wolf Road wakes," she says. "Viterran tax men are asking which passes you used. The clans are counting your steps. Speak true, or the cairn will add your name to its tally. And mind the Aeler outcast in the smithy---he's been here forty years and still forgets to duck under doorframes. He counts his breaths in eights. You should too. If you hear a woman washing clothes by the ford at midnight, do not ask her name. She is the bean-nighe, and she is washing the shrouds of the dead."

[colback=black!3,colframe=blue!60!black,title={Theme & Atmosphere,breakable] Ubral is a land of knife-edged ridges, mist-veiled glens, dark lochs, and stone cairns that mark the bones of old kings. Caught between the Aelerian mountains to the north and the lowland courts of Viterra to the south, its hills are scarred by centuries of raiding and reprisal. The clans speak a tongue thick with swallowed consonants and older grudges. They measure wealth in cattle, honor in wergild, and time in the generations since a Viterran banner last burned in the high passes. To the east, the gray waters of the Dolmis Sea crash against rocky shores, where fishing villages pay tribute to whichever clan controls the nearest pass---and sometimes to a different one each season.

Exiled Aeler---dwarves who could not abide the Deep Vaults' suffocating tradition---have found refuge here, sharing their cunning with forge and toll-road, and their outcast bitterness with any clan willing to listen. The lowland lords of Viterra claim suzerainty over Ubral; the clans call those claims "ink on wet stone." Every pass holds a watch-fire, every ford a memory of a battle, every hearth a knife waiting for a guest who stays too long.

The clans do not fight for land. They fight for honor---a geometry of obligation that stretches back centuries. An insult is not forgiven; it is weighed. A wound is not healed; it is counted against a blood-price. Every death opens a new angle in the feud, every reconciliation closes one---but the shape is never forgotten. The Aeler outcasts, with their breath-counters and bell-codes, understand this geometry better than most. They call it Kon'reh: the game of exits and obligations, where the ninth move is never made.

The old folk still speak of the sluagh---the host of the unforgiven dead that flies through the night sky, snatching souls who have no cairn to call their own. They whisper of the baobhan sith, the blood-drinking woman who dances with travelers before draining them dry, and the each uisge, the water horse that lures the unwary onto its back and drags them into the loch. The clans leave offerings of iron nails at fords to ward off the kelpies, and they never cut the ninth stalk of heather for fear of waking the uruisg, the wild spirit of the glen.

What you notice first: The smell of peat smoke and wet granite. The constant lowing of cattle on distant hills. The way every threshold has a knife hidden nearby---for guests, not against them. The way old men trace patterns in the dirt with sticks, arguing over angles that only they can see.

The one rule that kills newcomers: Never refuse a cup from a hearth-aunt. Hospitality is a contract sealed in salt and bread. Refusal is an insult that blood must answer. And never count to nine in a cairn's shadow. The dead are counting with you, and they stop at eight.

*{What Players Actually Do Here

The Ubral highlands are about honor, feud, and survival. A party in Ubral will most often:

itemize Settle or escalate feuds between clans, using wergild, guest-right, and cairn votes. Travel through contested passes, negotiating tolls with Aeler outcasts and avoiding reivers. Deal with the dead---cairns that speak, ghosts that count, and the Hollow's attention. Choose between honor and pragmatism, with consequences that echo through generations. itemize

Key hooks: itemize The Wolf Road wakes. Viterran tax men are asking which passes you used. Which clan will you offend? A Mac Tern groom cannot pay the wergild for an O'Creagh bride. The bride has fled to the Pass of Ashes. A cairn on Three-Fires Ridge is weeping water that tastes of salt. The dead are agitated. itemize

[colback=black!3,colframe=red!60!black,title={The Ninth Taboo Manifestation,breakable] In Ubral, the ninth stone in any cairn is always warm. Touch it, and you will remember an oath you never swore. Those who touch the ninth stone three times are said to become a cairn themselves---silent, still, and waiting for the next hand. The Aeler outcasts say the ninth stone is a door to the Deep Vaults they fled. They do not touch it. They count to eight and leave.

The ninth step on any path that leads to a loch is said to belong to the each uisge. Those who step on it hear the sound of hooves beneath the water and feel a cold breath on their neck. If they take a ninth step into the water, the water horse will claim them.

*{Regional Mood: Cairn, Tor, and the Geometry of Grudges

Here, the past is never past. Every cairn holds a voice, every burn scar on a hillside remembers a raid. Viterra has tried to conquer Ubral a dozen times; each attempt ended with supply lines cut by reivers, passes sealed by Aeler outcast engineers, and lowland knights lost in mist. The Utaran Imperium itself broke its leg on these hills and limped away. Now the clans watch the lowlands with patient eyes, and the exiled Aeler of Khaz-Vurim weigh every traveler's honor on iron scales. A guest here is kin---until they prove otherwise. And kin here will die for you---or kill you over a stolen cow. The Lady of Tor rules by consent of the Council of Cairns, and the Council speaks for the dead as much as the living. Every nine years, a moot is called at Gray Tor to settle the great feud---a dispute between the two oldest clans that no one can remember the origin of, but everyone remembers the blood.

The clans do not write their laws. They carve them---into stone, into memory, into the bones of their enemies. A feud is not a quarrel; it is a balance, an equation that must be solved in cattle or blood. A clan chief who cannot count the weight of an insult is a chief who will not last the winter. The Aeler outcasts, with their strange obsession for eights and their fear of the ninth breath, have found kindred spirits here. The clans count in eights too---eight cattle for a wound, eight winters for a grudge, eight generations for a blood-feud. The ninth is the Hollow's share.

Along the eastern coast, the sea cliffs drop sheer into the Dolmis. Fisherfolk in black-hulled boats speak of drowned cairns beneath the waves---ancient stones that mark the graves of clans who lost everything to the sea. Some say the Hollow rises from those waters on the ninth wave of the ninth tide. The fisherfolk do not go out on the ninth tide. They tie knots in their nets and wait. In the dark glens, the bean-sidhe (banshee) keens when a chieftain is about to die. The clans do not fear her; they listen, for her cry is the only honest herald of what is to come.

*{The Clans of Ubral (A Highland Primer)

[colback=black!3,colframe=blue!60!black,title={The Great Houses of the Stone,breakable]

tabular{|p{0.18|p{0.18|p{0.22|p{0.22|

Clan & Homeland & Feuds (Ancient) & Relationship with Viterra

Mac Tern & Gray Tor, upper Glen Tern & O'Creagh (blood feud) & Defiant; has never paid a lowland tax. Their cairn has nine notches---eight for victories, one waiting.

O'Creagh & Black Broom Bog, eastern fells & Mac Tern, Mac Gowan & Wary; accepts bribes but no garrisons. Their singers still compose verses about the cattle raid of '03.

Mac Gowan & Khaz-Vurim Steps, western passes & O'Creagh & Pragmatic; trades iron for lowland silver. Their smiths learned the counting song from Aeler outcasts.

O'Ruarc & Bride's Causey, southern marches & None active & Collaborator; factor in Valora is a cousin. The other clans call them "the lowland shadow."

Mac Taggart & Three-Fires Ridge, northern heights & Mac Tern (old) & Neutral; sells beacon service to both sides. Their fires burn in eights---never nine.

O'Cahan & Pass of Ashes, far west & None & Isolationist; speaks to lowlanders only through Aeler outcasts. They keep the old cairns that no one else will tend.

tabular

The Mac Tern--O'Creagh feud has outlasted every lowland king. Each has a cairn for every generation lost. No wergild has ever settled it; no marriage has ever bridged it. The Lady of Tor keeps the peace by reminding both that a Viterran judge would hang them all---and that the Hollow would thank her for it.

*{Faction Timers (Campaign Engines)

itemize The Viterran Yoke [6] -- Lowland crown's creeping control. Advances when a chief accepts a Viterran title, the party enforces lowland law, or a garrison is established. Retreats when a tax collector is sent home empty (or headless) or a claim is exposed as fraud. On full: a Queen's Justiciar arrives with a circuit court; lowland law becomes default. The cairns fall silent---the dead are watching. Tor's Hold [6] -- Lady Uria's personal authority. Advances when she wins a moot, brokers peace, or gains an ally. Retreats when she loses a feud or a client clan defects. On 0: she is deposed; civil war follows. The ninth cairn will have a new stone. Ancestral Grudge [8] -- The dead's patience. Advances when oaths are broken, wergild unpaid, or cairns defiled. On 4: the dead grow restless---navigation rolls in Ubral gain +1 DV. On 8: a cairn-wight (an undead guardian of the stones) rises and demands blood. The dead do not negotiate. They count. The Forge Weight [6] -- Khaz-Vurim's economic leverage. Advances when they secure toll contracts, sell iron, or gain a clan's weight of obligation. On full: they can close a pass to enforce terms. Their iron is cold, but their memory is colder. The Deep Road [8] -- Aeler outcasts' secret ambition. Advances as they survey, bribe, or blast. On 4: they find a promising route. On 8: construction begins on a dwarf under-way that would destroy several clan cairns. The mountain will remember. The party can stop it by exposing their plans, sabotaging their survey, or offering a better route. Mac Tern's Revenge [6] -- Advances when Mac Tern suffers an insult or loses cattle. On 4: they call a moot to plan a raid. On 6: they launch a devastating raid. The raiders count their steps in eights and leave a single white stone at each burned stead---a promise. O'Creagh's Patience [6] -- Retreats when O'Creagh is wronged. When it hits 0, they strike without warning. Their war-cry is a single word: Remember. itemize

*{Wergild Price Table (Quick Reference) [colback=black!3,colframe=black!40!white,title={Blood Prices (in cattle, unless noted),breakable]

tabular{|l|l|

Insult (spoken) & 1 cow

Stolen sheep & 3 cows

Broken betrothal & 5 cows + a horse

Wounding (by severity) & 7--15 cows

Killing a clansman & 21 cows + a public apology

Killing a chief & 42 cows + a hostage

Breaking an oath sworn on a cairn & 9 cows---one for each face of the stone

Killing a guest under your roof & No wergild. Exile or death. The Hollow will collect.

tabular

*{The Hollow's Cairn (Legendary Site) Hidden in the high mist, a ninth stone circle with no clan markings. The dead do not vote here; they listen. Once per arc, a character may climb the cairn and ask one question of the Hollow. The answer is always a weight.

Mechanics: Spirit + Resolve (DV 6). Success: the GM answers truthfully (one sentence). Failure: the character gains the Condition Marked by the Hollow (the GM may spend 2 SB to have an Oath-Keeper appear). If the character counts to nine while on the cairn, the Hollow answers without being asked---and takes a memory.

*{Starting Hooks itemize The Wolf Road wakes: Viterran tax men are asking which passes you used. The clans are counting your steps. One clan has offered a bounty for the tax men's heads---in cattle, not coin. The Bride-Price Default: A Mac Tern groom cannot pay the wergild for an O'Creagh bride. The bride has fled to the Pass of Ashes. Retrieve her---or pay her price yourself. The groom has offered his sword. The bride's clan wants his head. The Toll-Master's Burden: Khaz-Vurim has doubled tolls on the Steps. A clan chief suspects they are skimming to fund the Deep Road. Infiltrate the counting-house and find the hidden reckoning. The toll-master counts in eights. Do not let him reach nine. The Cairn that Weeps: A shepherd reports that a cairn on Three-Fires Ridge is weeping water that tastes of salt. The dead are agitated. Find out why before the clan blames a rival. The water has a rhythm---eight drips, a pause, eight drips. Something is counting. The Kelpie's Ford: A child has gone missing near the ford of Loch Dubh. The elders say the each uisge has taken her. They offer a fortune in cattle to whoever can retrieve the child---or drive the water horse back to its dark loch. itemize

*{Folk-Horror Bestiary of Ubral

The glens and lochs of Ubral are home to spirits older than the clans. Use these entries when the party wanders too far from the fire.

longtable{|p{0.2|p{0.3|p{0.3|

Creature & Signs & Hook

Each Uisge (Water Horse) & Wet hoofprints steaming in moonlight; a beautiful horse or shaggy man by the loch. & Offer it a bridle of cold iron, and it will serve you once---then try to drown you.

Bean-Nighe (Washer at the Ford) & A woman washing shrouds at a ford at midnight. She has no face---only a hood of gray water. & If you see her, you have one day to live unless you can answer her riddle. She will trade a death omen for a story about a death you caused.

Baobhan Sith & A beautiful woman in a green dress who dances with lone travelers. She has deer hooves instead of feet. & She offers a dance that grants +1 die to seduction or deception, but marks you as her prey. Iron burns her.

Sluagh (Host of the Unforgiven) & A flock of gray, bird-like spirits flying through the night sky, snatching souls without cairns. & They fear the sound of a bell. They may offer to carry a message to the dead for a memory.

Cù Sìth (Fairy Dog) & A great green hound with a braided tail, harbinger of death. & Three barks mean you will die before sunset; one bark means you will outlive your enemies. It may adopt a character who shows mercy to a wounded animal.

Cairn-Wight & An undead guardian of the stones, risen when Ancestral Grudge fills. & It does not negotiate. It counts. Offer a wergild in cattle or a true regret to send it back.

longtable

*{Prominent NPCs of Ubral

longtable{|p{0.2|p{0.25|p{0.3|p{0.15|

Name & Role/Title & Motivation & Rumoured Quirk

Lady Uria & Clan-chief of the Gray Tor & Keep the old oaths and resist Viterran encroachment & She lost a hand to a Viterran knight; her iron hook has a built-in bell. It rings when she lies. It has never rung.

Hearth-Aunt Brigid & Keeper of guest-right (commoner exemplar) & Protect her household and settle feuds before blood is spilled & She has never drawn a blade. She has never needed to. Her hospitality has ended more feuds than swords.

Councilor Gethin & Elder of the Council of Cairns & Preserve the wergild system and the memory of the dead & He speaks once a year; his sentences take a full season to deliver. He has never spoken a ninth word.

Oath-Singer Lilias & Verse-weaver and feud mediator & Turn vengeance into cattle, not corpses & Her tongue is forked---she can praise and curse in the same breath. Her songs are older than the Viterran crown.

Vent-Prior Orla & Aeler outcast leader of Khaz-Vurim & Build a new under-way through Ubral, linking mountains to lowlands & She carries a bell that rings once for each miner she left behind. She counts her breaths in eights---aloud.

Sister Niamh & Healer of the Cairn-Keepers of the Gray Palm (Temple of Light) & Run a free hospice at Gray Tor without angering the old gods & She asks no payment, but she writes every patient's name on a slate that never fills. The slate has eight columns.

Bean-Nighe & The Washer at the Ford (spirit omen) & Appear to those about to die, washing their burial shrouds & She has no face---only a hood of gray water. If you ask her name, she will show you your own death.

longtable

*{Cairn Vote (d8) When a cairn is asked to judge a dispute, roll 1d8:

tabular{|c|c|c|

d8 & Outcome & Mechanical Effect

1--2 & Dead vote for war & Advance the related Feud or Conflict timer by +2 segments. 3--4 & Dead are divided & No change, but the GM gains 1 SB (the cairn is watching). 5--6 & Dead vote for peace & Retreat the related timer by (-1) segment. 7 & Dead demand tribute & The cairn will not judge until an offering is made (1 Supply or a story). 8 & Dead vote for blood-price & The losing party must pay a wergild of 3 cows (1 Supply) or the feud continues.

tabular

*{Guest-Right Violations (d6) When a PC breaks guest-right (refuses a cup, insults a host, draws steel at table), roll 1d6:

tabular{|c|l|

d6 & Consequence

1 & The host demands a wergild of 1 Supply immediately. 2 & The cairn nearest the hall votes against you---start a Grudge [4] timer. 3 & A witness spreads the story. Gain a Black Mark ((-1) die on all social rolls in Ubral for one session). 4 & The host's kin challenge you to a duel to first blood (Body+Melee, DV 3). The loser owes a story. 5 & The hearth-stone cracks. The hall is now Desperate for all social rolls until you make an offering (1 Supply or a story). 6 & The Hollow takes notice. GM gains 2 SB to spend on supernatural complications. The host's family will remember---for generations.

tabular
